name: sim_autograd_deep_nabla_Ic_admissibility -- autograd ∇I_c on rho admissibility probe

pytorch.reason: autograd is load-bearing: gradient of I_c through partial_trace+eigvalsh is computed only via autograd; finite-diff would change the claim

pyg.reason: graph message passing not required for this scalar/matrix-functional claim

z3.reason: claim is numerical-gradient structure, not SMT admissibility (deferred to companion proof sim)

cvc5.reason: same as z3: numerical gradient claim, not SMT

sympy.reason: symbolic form of entropy/Fisher/KL is standard; numerical gradient is the load-bearing object

clifford.reason: no Cl(n) rotor needed for scalar functional of rho

geomstats.reason: manifold metric not used; Fisher computed directly via autograd

rustworkx.reason: no graph structure in this probe

toponetx.reason: no cell complex in this probe

gudhi.reason: no persistent homology in this probe

scope_note: Autograd ∇I_c on 2-qubit rho; admissibility = non-zero gradient norm on entangled candidate, zero on product.

entangled_candidate_nonzero_grad.note: candidate survived admissibility probe (nonzero autograd gradient)

product_state_excluded_from_entangled_cl.note: product candidate excluded: I_c indistinguishable from 0

near_maximally_mixed_finite_gradient.note: boundary candidate: gradient remains finite under autograd
